$NOW is expanding its $MSFT partnership by integrating AI Control Tower with Microsoft Agent 365.

The goal is to give enterprises unified visibility, approval workflows and policy controls for AI agents across both the ServiceNow and Microsoft ecosystems.

Just realized the gains from my $DOCN position alone could now officially pay for all 4 years of college for my son (who is being born this month).

I love this game  😅

$CIFR just broke above $20 after securing 4.2 GW of power capacity and signing another hyperscaler deal tied to its AI data center buildout.

Management says the company is “transforming into an energy, data center and HPC infrastructure company.”
SEC just proposed letting public companies report earnings twice a year instead of quarterly.

Anthropic’s CEO said AI could bankrupt SaaS companies even if software keeps growing overall while claiming Claude is still 6-12 months ahead of Chinese models.

He also said Anthropic’s annualized revenue grew 80x in Q1, headcount is now ~3,500 and its security work has likely found tens of thousands of unknown vulnerabilities.

$OUST expanded its $NVDA partnership to bring Rev8 digital lidar integrations across the Nvidia Jetson platform.

Ouster is giving robots better “eyes” with color-aware 3D sensing helping them see, map and understand the physical world in real time.
These 2027 $MU LEAPS I bought just 5 weeks ago are now up more than 5x  🤯
$DOCN UP OVER 30% AND HITTING A NEW ALL-TIME HIGH  🙃

$MU is shipping the world’s first 245TB data center SSD giving AI data centers much more storage in far less space.

Micron says the drive can reduce storage racks by 82%, use far less power than hard drives and lower costs as AI workloads keep growing.
$GOOGL MAKES $1M IN PROFIT IN UNDER 4 MINUTES  🤯

All portfolio performance & transaction data have been independently validated by an independent third party:

savvytrader.com/StockSavvySh…

Wild to see my initial $DOCN position up ~5x in less than a year after spending the summer building in the $20s around a thesis nobody was talking about. 

Analysts expected ~13% growth but I thought AI inference could drive a major reacceleration by 2027 and today DigitalOcean just guided to 50% growth. 

Conviction before the market sees it is the whole game.

$PLTR is calling itself a “no slop zone” after replacing legacy tools like CRM internally and positioning itself against thin software that charges rent without delivering outcomes.

Karp summed it up best when he said “the appearance of software working is not software working” which is why real AI value comes from platforms that actually hold up in high-stakes environments.

$DUOL sits in one of the clearest categories for AI disruption as $GOOGL Gemini, ChatGPT & Claude make real-time language tutoring easier to access.

The company has to prove its gamified habit loop, data & product design can still keep users engaged as AI tutors improve.

The risk is that Duolingo becomes more of an AI learning wrapper than the primary interface but with the stock down ~75% &trading under 15x earnings, a lot of that fear may already be priced in.

Iran is jamming the AIS (or GPS) signals of shipping. Notice what happens to it below, and how they jump around and then disappear.

Signaling no restart to the war?

No War
No Peace
No Shipping
No Oil, LNG, Fertilizer, or Helium.

Oil's impact on the bond market

I would argue that oil (blue) is driving bond volatility (orange), not the other way around.

That means wider daily ranges and, given the current environment, higher yields (>volatility = higher yields).

What does the oil market think?

Both the July (orange) and December (blue) Brent Crude Oil Futures Contracts are at new highs.

The December contract is now signaling that the market expects crude oil to be above $90 for the next seven months.
The 30-year yield is now 8 bps away from a new 18-year high.

Seems like $aapl on its way to make ATH’s now that the post earnings pivot support was defined

📺 A LITTLE DIGESTION WON’T HURT NOBODY + TRIM MEMORY STRENGTH

The market isn’t breaking — it’s digesting. After a strong run, both $SPY, $QQQ, and small caps $IWM are simply moving sideways while holding key short-term support (the 8-day moving average).

That’s constructive, not bearish. Strength is shown by the fact that tech didn’t even pull back to the 8-day — it just paused. That’s underlying demand.

*

Most traders lose because they focus on macro noise (war headlines) instead of price action. The right move here was simple — reduce hedges into strength, buy dips near support, and trim into rebounds. 

*

You need to follow the trend — no exceptions. I keep saying that stocks that are above the 8-day and trending higher — like leaders $MU and $SNDK — should be treated as longs, not shorts. 

Fighting strong trends because something “feels overvalued” is a losing game.

Here is the $SNDK example. The correct entries were:
– When it reclaimed a key level after weakness (showing buyers stepping back in)
– When it broke prior highs (confirmation of momentum)

Once it’s already extended and up multiple days in a row, you don’t chase — you trim into strength, not initiate new buys.

Same with $MU — continued upside while the market pauses = relative strength. That’s where money is being made.

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet
$SNDK gave everyone ways to adjust/manage and get involved again post earnings Friday

24/24 

CHARACTER STATS:

Cohen — Speed: Calm. Strength: Vibes. Special Move: Half Cash Half Stock.

Hodlster — Speed: Robinhood Latency. Special Move: Mortgage The Wheel.

23/24

COMING NEXT:

The Roaring Kitty Returns.

Plus: will TD Securities answer the phone?

Sunset over the GameStop+eBay merged castle.

The Amazon truck, still unbothered, in the distance.

22/24

Letter from Hodlster:

"Dear Mr. Cohen,

I sold my hamster wheel for GME calls. Please complete the deal by Friday.

Yours in diamond hands,
Hodlster

P.S. Tell Andrew of Squawk I said hi."

21/24

WHAT WE LEARNED TODAY:

The Meme King has chips. He will play them.

Do not interrupt a CEO mid-mantra.

Half cash + half stock + half vibes = 1.5x belief.

The math doesn't have to work for the meme to work.

Highly Confident is a vibe, not a contract.

20/24

The prediction markets weighed in.

Kalshi: "GameStop completes eBay acquisition by 2026"

YES: 26%
NO: 74%
Volume: $2,143

The market gave it a 1-in-4 shot.

The market is funded by people who can't smell vibes.

19/24

The apes united.

Pitchforks made of diamond-hand emojis.

Banner: "EBAY OR DIE."

Hodlster front and center: "IF WE BUY ENOUGH GME CALLS THE DEAL FUNDS ITSELF."

(memetic finance: 0/1 attempts so far)

18/24

Hodlster mortgaged his hamster wheel.

Put it up as collateral.

"GAMESTOP IS BUYING AMAZON. WAIT. EBAY. SAME THING. ALL IN."

The retail army mobilized.

17/24

Cut to Amazon HQ.

Jeff is on a beach.

Cocktail in hand.

Watching this all unfold on a tablet.

Laughing.

The actual Amazon Killer was at Cabo. Unworried.

16/24

This is the famous "Highly Confident Letter" from TD Securities.

It's a yellow Post-it note.

It says: "we feel pretty good about it — the boys @ TD"

NOTE: highly confident is NOT the same as committed. Or signed. Or real.

15/24

PLOT TWIST.

GameStop already owned 5% of eBay.

They forgot to mention it earlier.

Hodlster: "WAIT WHAT."

Quietly building. The Meme King has chips.

14/24

Meanwhile in San Jose.

eBay's board, surrounded by Beanie Babies and PEZ dispensers, panicking.

CFO: "They want to buy us... WITH VIBES?!"

eBay was not consulted. eBay was perplexed.

13/24

And then he leaned back.

Arms behind his head.

Smile of pure unbothered chaos.

"Yeah. We'll see what happens."

That was the moment the market realized.

He's actually doing this.

12/24

HALF CASH.
HALF STOCK.
HALF CASH.
HALF STOCK.
HALF CASH.
HALF STOCK.

Andrew: "Sir. Please."

Cohen, sunglasses on, arms crossed, infinite patience.

A masterclass in not answering the question.
